admin.title: Administration
admin.resetConfirm: All stored HA settings will be deleted. Values from environment variables will be used again.
admin.haSectionHelper: Connect Kamerplanter to your Home Assistant instance for sensor and automation data.

activities.title: Activities
activities.name: Name
activities.stressLevelHint: How much stress does this activity cause to the plant?
activities.recoveryDaysHint: Days the plant needs to recover after this activity.
activities.sortOrderHint: Determines display order in lists. Lower values appear first.
activities.systemHint: System activities can be edited but not deleted.
activities.forbiddenPhasesHint: Growth phases during which this activity must not be performed (e.g. flowering, germination).
activities.restrictedSubPhasesHint: Sub-phases where this activity should be used with caution (e.g. late_flower).
activities.toolsRequiredHint: Tools or aids needed to perform this activity.
activities.requiresPhotoHint: A photo is required when completing the task.
activities.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the activity "{{name}}"?
activities.speciesCompatibleHint: Empty = universal for all species. Add entries to restrict this activity to specific species.
activities.scopeUniversalInfo: This activity is universal and available for all plant species.
activities.scopeRestrictedInfo: This activity is restricted to {{count}} species/names.
activities.sectionIdentificationDesc: Name and description of the activity in both languages.
activities.sectionClassificationDesc: Category, difficulty level and impact on the plant.
activities.sectionExecutionDesc: Time required, tools needed and documentation requirements.
activities.sectionScopeDesc: Defines whether the activity is available for all or only specific species.
activities.sectionPhaseRestrictionsDesc: Growth phases where this activity is forbidden or restricted.
activities.sectionMetaDesc: Free-text tags and sort order for display in lists.
activities.editIntro: Edit the properties of this activity. Changes affect future tasks.
activities.listIntro: Activities define actions that can be performed on plants (e.g. pruning, transplanting, training). System activities are pre-populated.

auth.registrationSuccess: Registration successful! Please check your email.
auth.verificationSuccess: Email successfully verified! You can now log in.
auth.resetEmailSent: If an account with this email exists, a reset link has been sent.

smartHome.title: Smart Home & Sensors
smartHome.description: Enable this option if you want to use smart home devices, sensors or actuators with Kamerplanter.
smartHome.disabledInfo: Smart Home features are disabled. Sensor and actuator elements will be hidden throughout the interface.

auth.platformModeSubtitle: Choose the operating mode for your Kamerplanter instance. Changes require updating the environment variable and a restart.
auth.platformModeLightDescription: For single users and small groups on a local network. No login, no organizations, no GDPR consent banners. Instant access after opening.
auth.platformModeLightFeatures: No login required;One system user for everyone;No tenant management;No GDPR settings;Ideal for Raspberry Pi, home server, localhost
auth.platformModeFullDescription: Multi-user operation with full authentication, tenant management, roles, and GDPR compliance. Suitable for teams, community gardens, and SaaS deployments.
auth.platformModeFullFeatures: User registration & login;Multiple organizations & gardens;Role-based access control;GDPR privacy & consent;API keys & sessions
auth.platformModeEnterpriseDescription: Advanced features for professional and commercial use. Includes all Full Mode features plus enhanced administration and integration capabilities.
auth.platformModeEnterpriseFeatures: All Full Mode features;Advanced audit logs;OIDC/SSO integration;Multi-cluster support;Priority support & SLA
auth.platformModeEnvHint: Set the environment variable KAMERPLANTER_MODE to the desired value and restart the application.
auth.adminDeleteTenantConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete the organization "{{name}}" and all its memberships?
auth.adminDeleteUserConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete user "{{name}}" ({{email}}) and all associated data?
auth.experienceLevelSubtitle: Adjust the displayed UI complexity to match your experience.
auth.experienceLevelDowngradeWarning: Downgrading will hide some fields and navigation entries. Your data will be preserved. Continue?

experienceLevel.beginnerDescription: Simple view with essential fields only

auth.wateringCanSizeHelper: Default volume of your watering can for dosage calculation.
auth.rerunOnboardingDescription: Restart the setup wizard to add a new scenario.
auth.passwordChanged: Password changed. All sessions have been terminated.
auth.deleteAccountWarning: Deleting your account cannot be undone. All data will be permanently removed.
auth.deleteAccountConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete your account? This cannot be undone.
auth.rememberMeTooltip: Only enable on private devices. Your session will stay active for up to 30 days.
auth.apiKeyCreatedWarning: Copy the key now — it will not be shown again!
auth.profileSectionDesc: Display name and email address of your account.
auth.regionSectionDesc: Preferred language and timezone for date display.
auth.securitySectionDesc: Change or set your local password.
auth.emailReadOnly: Your email address cannot be changed here.
auth.deleteAccountDescription: Your account and all your data will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.

tenants.createIntro: Create a new organization to share your garden with others.
tenants.name: Name
tenants.invitationAccepted: Invitation accepted! You are now a member.

botanicalFamilies.title: Botanical Families
botanicalFamilies.listIntro: Botanical families group plant species by relationship and determine typical nutrient requirements. Click an entry to see details and associated species.
botanicalFamilies.createIntro: Create a new botanical family. Families group plant species with similar nutrient requirements.
botanicalFamilies.editIntro: Edit the properties of this botanical family. Changes affect all associated species.
botanicalFamilies.name: Name
botanicalFamilies.nameHelper: Botanical family name, must end with '-aceae' (e.g. 'Solanaceae')
botanicalFamilies.orderHelper: Taxonomic order, must end with '-ales' if provided (e.g. 'Solanales')
botanicalFamilies.descriptionHelper: Brief botanical description of the family
botanicalFamilies.nutrientDemandHelper: Typical nutrient requirements: Light feeder, Medium feeder or Heavy feeder
botanicalFamilies.nitrogenFixingHelper: Nitrogen-fixing families (e.g. Fabaceae) reduce fertilizer needs. Incompatible with Heavy feeder.
botanicalFamilies.rootDepthHelper: Typical root depth: Shallow (<30 cm), Medium (30–60 cm), Deep (>60 cm)
botanicalFamilies.soilPhHelper: pH range preference for optimal growth (3.0–9.0)
botanicalFamilies.frostToleranceHelper: Frost tolerance level of the family
botanicalFamilies.growthFormsHelper: Typical growth forms in the family, select at least one
botanicalFamilies.commonPestsHelper: Common pests for this family (press Enter to add)
botanicalFamilies.commonDiseasesHelper: Common fungal or bacterial diseases (press Enter to add)
botanicalFamilies.pollinationTypeHelper: Pollination types for this family, select at least one
botanicalFamilies.sectionTaxonomyDesc: Scientific classification and naming of the family.
botanicalFamilies.sectionGrowthDesc: Typical nutrient demand, root depth and growth forms of family members.
botanicalFamilies.sectionEnvironmentDesc: Preferred soil pH range and frost tolerance of the family.
botanicalFamilies.sectionPestsAndDiseasesDesc: Common pests and diseases within this family.
botanicalFamilies.sectionReproductionDesc: Pollination types and crop rotation classification.
botanicalFamilies.noSpeciesInFamily: No species have been assigned to this family yet.
botanicalFamilies.showAllSpeciesFiltered: Show all species of this family

species.title: Species
species.listIntro: Plant species form the foundation of master data. Each species belongs to a botanical family and can have multiple cultivars.
species.createIntro: Create a new plant species. The scientific name must follow binomial nomenclature (e.g. 'Solanum lycopersicum').
species.editIntro: Edit the properties of this plant species. Changes affect associated cultivars and lifecycle configurations.
species.scientificNameHelper: Binomial nomenclature: Genus + specific epithet (e.g. 'Solanum lycopersicum')
species.commonNamesHelper: Common names in any language (press Enter to add)
species.familyHelper: Associated botanical family — determines crop rotation and nutrient requirements
species.genusHelper: Botanical genus, first part of the scientific name (e.g. 'Solanum')
species.growthHabitHelper: Typical growth form: herb, shrub, tree, vine or groundcover
species.rootTypeHelper: Root system: fibrous, taproot, tuberous or bulbous
species.hardinessZonesHelper: USDA hardiness zones (e.g. '7a', '8b') — press Enter to add
species.nativeHabitatHelper: Original distribution area of the species (e.g. 'South America, Andes')
species.allelopathyScoreHelper: Biochemical interaction: −1 (strongly inhibiting) to +1 (strongly promoting), 0 = neutral
species.baseTempHelper: Threshold temperature for GDD calculation (Growing Degree Days), typically 5–15 °C
species.descriptionHelper: Brief botanical description of the species
species.synonymsHelper: Taxonomic synonyms / former scientific names (press Enter to add)
species.taxonomicAuthorityHelper: Original describer of the species per ICZN/ICN (e.g. 'L.' for Linnaeus)
species.taxonomicStatusHelper: Current status: accepted, synonym, doubtful, misapplied
species.containerSuitableHelper: Suitability for permanent container culture: Yes, Limited or No
species.recommendedContainerVolumeLHelper: Recommended container volume in liters (e.g. '10--20' for a range)
species.minContainerDepthCmHelper: Minimum container depth in centimeters for healthy root growth
species.matureHeightCmHelper: Expected height of the mature plant (e.g. '100--300')
species.matureWidthCmHelper: Expected width of the mature plant (e.g. '80--150')
species.spacingCmHelper: Recommended spacing between plants in outdoor beds (e.g. '50--80')
species.indoorSuitableHelper: Suitability for year-round indoor growing (windowsill, grow tent, indoors)
species.balconySuitableHelper: Suitability for growing on a balcony or terrace (seasonal or year-round)
species.greenhouseRecommendedHelper: Does the plant benefit from protected growing in a greenhouse?
species.supportRequiredHelper: Does the plant need a trellis, stake or support structure?
species.defaultNutrientPlanHelper: Used as fallback for plants of this species when no individual plan is assigned
species.noWorkflows: No workflows for this species yet. Auto-generate one or create one manually.
species.duplicateWorkflowDescription: Creates a copy of "{{name}}" with all task templates. You can then customize the copy.
species.growingPeriodsHelper: Drag bar edges to adjust sowing and harvest/bloom months. Click a period to edit details.
species.sowingIndoorWeeksHelper: Weeks before the last frost date for indoor pre-sowing
species.sowingOutdoorDaysHelper: Days after the last frost for direct sowing/transplanting
species.legacySowingFieldsHelper: These fields are only used when no growing periods are defined.
species.dragBarsHint: Drag bars above to set sowing/harvest/bloom months
species.harvestFromYearHelper: From which year the plant can be harvested (empty = from year 1)
species.bloomFromYearHelper: From which year the plant blooms (empty = from year 1)

cultivars.title: Cultivars
cultivars.editIntro: Edit the properties of this cultivar. Changes affect associated plant instances.
cultivars.name: Name
cultivars.breederHelper: Name of the breeder or seed company
cultivars.breedingYearHelper: Year of cultivar registration or first description
cultivars.patentStatusHelper: Protection status: e.g. 'PVP', 'Patent', 'Open Source', empty = unknown
cultivars.daysToMaturityHelper: Typical days from planting to harvest (1–365)
cultivars.phaseWateringOverridesHelper: Cultivar-specific watering intervals per growth phase. Overrides the species default. Empty fields use the species default.
cultivars.sectionIdentificationDesc: Name, breeder and origin of the cultivar.

companionPlanting.title: Companion Planting
companionPlanting.intro: Companion planting describes which plant species benefit or inhibit each other. Select a species to view and edit its compatibility relationships.
companionPlanting.reasonHelper: Brief description of why this combination is unfavorable
companionPlanting.selectSpeciesHelper: Select a species to view its companion planting relationships
companionPlanting.selectSpeciesHint: Please select a species to view companion planting relationships.
companionPlanting.addCompatibleHint: Specify which species works well with the selected species.
companionPlanting.addIncompatibleHint: Specify which species does not work well with the selected species.
companionPlanting.targetSpeciesHelper: Species to be related to the selected species

cropRotation.title: Crop Rotation
cropRotation.intro: Crop rotation defines which botanical family should follow another and how long the waiting period is.
cropRotation.addSuccessorHint: Specify which family can be planted after the selected family.
cropRotation.waitYearsHelper: Minimum waiting years before the same family can be planted again (1–10)
cropRotation.fromFamilyHelper: Select the source family to view successor recommendations
cropRotation.toFamilyHelper: Family that can be planted after the source family
cropRotation.selectFamilyHint: Please select a family to view crop rotation recommendations.

sites.title: Sites
sites.noRuns: No planting runs at this site yet
sites.name: Name
sites.totalAreaHelper: Total growing area in square meters
sites.editIntro: Edit the basic data of this site. Name and climate zone help organize locations.
sites.sectionBasicDataDesc: Name, climate zone, area, and timezone of the site.

water.ghPlausibilityWarning: General hardness deviates from Ca/Mg values
water.measurementAgeWarning: Water measurement is older than 12 months
water.roMembraneWarning: RO membrane may need replacement (EC too high)

locations.title: Locations
locations.editIntro: Edit the properties of this location. Lighting and irrigation settings define growing conditions.
locations.sectionBasicDataDesc: Name and area of the location.
locations.sectionLightingIrrigationDesc: Light source, irrigation system, and light schedule for this location.
locations.name: Name
locations.areaHelper: Usable area of the location in square meters
locations.noPlantsOrRuns: No plants or runs at this location

locationTypes.title: Location Types
locationTypes.name: Name
locationTypes.cannotDeleteSystem: System location types cannot be deleted

slots.title: Slots
slots.slotIdHelper: Format: LOCATION_POSITION, e.g. 'TENT01_A1' (automatically converted to uppercase)
slots.capacityHelper: Maximum number of plants per slot (1–20)

substrates.title: Substrates
substrates.editIntro: Edit the properties of this substrate. Changes affect associated batches.
substrates.typeHelper: Substrate type determines water retention and buffer capacity
substrates.name: Name
substrates.ecBaseHelper: Electrical conductivity of the base substrate in mS/cm
substrates.waterRetentionHelper: Ability of the substrate to retain water
substrates.airPorosityHelper: Percentage of air-filled pores in the substrate (0–100%)
substrates.bufferCapacityHelper: Ability of the substrate to buffer pH fluctuations
substrates.reusableHelper: Whether the substrate can be reused after treatment
substrates.maxReuseCyclesHelper: Maximum number of reuses before disposal
substrates.sectionIdentificationDesc: Type, brand, and name of the substrate in both languages.
substrates.sectionChemistryDesc: pH value and electrical conductivity of the untreated substrate.
substrates.sectionPhysicalDesc: Water retention, air porosity, and buffer capacity determine irrigation behavior.
substrates.sectionReuseDesc: Define whether and how often this substrate can be reused.
substrates.mixIntro: Combine existing substrates into a custom mix. Properties are calculated proportionally.
substrates.adjustFractions: Adjust the fractions so they sum to exactly 100%.

batches.title: Batches
batches.editIntro: Edit the properties of this substrate batch. Measurements (pH, EC, cycles) are tracked automatically.
batches.volumeHelper: Total volume of the batch in liters

plantInstances.title: Plant Instances
plantInstances.editIntro: Edit the associations of this plant. Instance ID and species cannot be changed.
plantInstances.plantNameHelper: Optional display name for this plant
plantInstances.sectionGeneralDesc: Name and cultivar of the plant.
plantInstances.sectionSetupDesc: Container volume, substrate, and planting date.
plantInstances.sectionLocationDesc: Site, area, and slot assignment for the plant.
plantInstances.cultivarKeyHelper: Select a cultivar for this species (optional)
plantInstances.substrateBatchKeyHelper: Key of the associated substrate batch
plantInstances.containerVolumeLitersHelper: Actual volume of the pot/container in liters
plantInstances.noNutrientPlanHint: No nutrient plan assigned — switch to the Nutrient Plan tab to assign one.
plantInstances.estimatedHarvestTooltip: Estimate based on planting date plus the sum of all typical phase durations. Actual harvest may vary depending on growth.
plantInstances.infoIntro: Overview of this plant instance: current location, growth phase, watering status, and linked master data.
plantInstances.wateringLogIntro: All watering and fertilization events for this plant. EC values after watering help monitor nutrient supply.
plantInstances.ecTooltip: Electrical Conductivity (EC, mS/cm): measures nutrient concentration in the solution. Higher value = more dissolved nutrients. Typical: 0.8–2.5 mS/cm depending on growth phase.
plantInstances.phTooltip: pH value: measures acidity of the nutrient solution. Affects nutrient availability. Optimal: 5.5–6.5 for most crops.
plantInstances.npkTooltip: NPK ratio: Nitrogen (N) – Phosphorus (P) – Potassium (K). Defines the ratio of primary nutrients in the fertilizer. Vegetative: N-heavy; Flowering: P/K-heavy.
plantInstances.ecAfterTooltip: EC value (Electrical Conductivity) of the runoff water after watering, in mS/cm. Indicates remaining nutrient concentration in the substrate.

tag.title: Create Tag / Label
tag.description: Create a QR code or write an NFC tag for "{{name}}".
tag.nfcNotSupported: Web NFC is not supported by this browser. Use Chrome on Android.
tag.nfcInstructions: Hold the NFC tag against the back of your smartphone and press the button.

phases.title: Phase Control
phases.targetPhaseHelper: Select the next phase — duration in days shown in parentheses
phases.forceHintCaption: Enable force correction if the phase was set incorrectly.
phases.forceCorrectionHint: Allows resetting to an earlier phase, e.g. when a plant was created in the wrong phase.
phases.deletePhaseMessage: Do you really want to delete this phase entry? The previous phase will be restored as the current phase.

lifecycle.title: Lifecycle Configuration
lifecycle.vernalizationDaysHelper: Minimum cold treatment duration in days
lifecycle.criticalDayLengthHelper: Day length that triggers flowering (0–24 h)

growthPhases.title: Growth Phases
growthPhases.name: Name
growthPhases.displayNameHelper: Optional display name (if empty: name is used)
growthPhases.durationHelper: Typical duration of this phase in days
growthPhases.sequenceOrderHelper: Position in the phase sequence (0 = first phase)
growthPhases.wateringIntervalHelper: Phase-specific watering interval — leave empty for default

profiles.noProfiles: No profile yet. Generate defaults or enter values manually.
profiles.editDesc: Adjust the environment and nutrient requirements for this growth phase.
profiles.requirementsDesc: Light, temperature, humidity, and irrigation parameters.
profiles.nutrientsDesc: NPK ratio, EC and pH targets, and trace elements.

plantingRuns.name: Name

tanks.sectionEquipmentDesc: Accessories and technical equipment of the tank.

tanks.reminderDaysBeforeHelper: Days before due date to send reminder

fertilizers.notUsedInAnyPlan: This fertilizer is not used in any nutrient plan.
fertilizers.ecContributionHelper: EC increase per ml fertilizer in 1 L water
fertilizers.tankSafeHelper: Can be pre-mixed and stored in tank
fertilizers.detailsIntro: Overview of all properties of this fertilizer – from nutrient profile to storage recommendations.
fertilizers.sectionNutrientsDetailIntro: NPK ratio, EC contribution and pH effect of this fertilizer.
fertilizers.sectionMixingDetailIntro: Application method, mixing priority and bioavailability.
fertilizers.sectionStorageDetailIntro: Recommended storage temperature and shelf life from purchase date.
fertilizers.stockIntro: Current stock entries and expiry dates for this fertilizer.
fertilizers.stockExpiringWarning: {{count}} stock entry expires within the next 30 days.
fertilizers.stockExpiringWarning_plural: {{count}} stock entries expire within the next 30 days.
fertilizers.stockEmptyDesc: Add the first stock entry for this fertilizer.
fertilizers.editIntro: Adjust the technical properties and storage data for this fertilizer.
fertilizers.sectionIdentificationIntro: Product name, brand and category of the fertilizer.
fertilizers.sectionNutrientsIntro: NPK ratio and electrical conductivity contribution to the nutrient solution.
fertilizers.sectionMixingIntro: Mixing order, pH effect and bioavailability in the tank.
fertilizers.sectionPropertiesIntro: Special properties such as organic certification and tank compatibility.
fertilizers.sectionStorageIntro: Storage temperature and minimum shelf life from purchase date.
fertilizers.sectionNotesIntro: Your own remarks, dosing notes or special characteristics.
fertilizers.mixingPriorityHelperFull: Lower number = mixed in earlier. CalMag (1) always before sulfates (2+).
fertilizers.maxDoseHelper: Maximum dosage per manufacturer (ml per liter of water)
fertilizers.ecUncertainHelper: Enable if the EC contribution is estimated or not specified by the manufacturer.
fertilizers.ecUncertainHint: The EC contribution of this fertilizer is estimated and may vary.
fertilizers.addStockIntro: Record a new stock entry for this fertilizer.

nutrientPlans.name: Name
nutrientPlans.author: Author

nutrientPlans.noFertilizers: No fertilizers assigned. Add fertilizers with dosages.

nutrientPlans.assignPlanIntro: Select a nutrient plan. Favorites are shown first.
nutrientPlans.noFavorites: No favorite nutrient plans. Mark plans as favorites in the nutrient plan list.

waterMix.noRecommendation: No recommendation available (no RO system or no target EC)
waterMix.noWaterConfig: To calculate RO water ratios, configure the water source on site "{{siteName}}" (RO system + tap water profile).

dosageCalc.intro: Calculates optimal dosages based on the water profile of the selected site. Reference dosages are automatically adjusted to your actual water quality.
dosageCalc.selectSiteFirst: Please select a site first to calculate dosages.
dosageCalc.noWaterProfile: No water profile on site. Add a water profile for automatic dosage adjustment.

nutrientPlans.sectionGeneralDesc: Name, description, substrate type, and metadata of the nutrient plan.
nutrientPlans.sectionAdvancedDesc: RO water ratio and cycle restart for perennial plants.
nutrientPlans.sectionScheduleDesc: Optional watering schedule with weekdays or interval, preferred time, and reminders.

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.queueIntro: Your pending tasks, grouped by urgency. Click a task to see details or perform actions.
tasks.workflowsIntro: Workflow templates define reusable task sequences for proven care strategies. Click a template to instantiate it.
tasks.createIntro: Create a new standalone task. Optionally select a plant and due date.
tasks.deleteNotSupported: Deletion is not supported for tasks.
tasks.noNewReminders: All care tasks are up to date.
tasks.noTasks: No tasks in the queue. Create a new task or instantiate a workflow.
tasks.noTemplates: No task templates in this workflow.
tasks.name: Name
tasks.completeIntro: Enter optional notes and actual duration to complete the task.
tasks.instantiateIntro: Select a plant to create the tasks from this workflow.
tasks.workflowInstantiated: Workflow instantiated successfully. Tasks have been created.
tasks.generateFromSpeciesDescription: Select a species to automatically generate an activity plan with appropriate care activities.
tasks.dayOffsetHelper: Days after phase entry when the activity should take place
tasks.editIntro: Edit the properties of this task. Name, category, scheduling, and assignment can be adjusted.
tasks.sectionTaskDesc: Name, instruction, and category of the task.
tasks.sectionSchedulingDesc: Due date, priority, estimated duration, and timer settings.
tasks.sectionCompletionDesc: Notes, actual duration, and ratings for completing the task.
tasks.priorityHelper: low = not urgent, critical = handle immediately
tasks.createWorkflowIntro: Create a new workflow to define reusable task sequences.
tasks.workflowEditIntro: Edit the name, description, version, and tags of this workflow.
tasks.recurrenceRuleHelper: e.g. '0 8 * * 1' for every Monday at 8 AM
tasks.assignedToHelper: User key of the assigned person
tasks.instructionHelper: Step-by-step instructions for completing the task

ipm.title: Plant Protection
ipm.pestListIntro: Pests are organisms that attack plants and cause crop losses. Manage your pest catalog here with scientific data.
ipm.diseaseListIntro: Plant diseases are caused by fungi, bacteria or viruses. Manage your disease catalog here with incubation periods and environmental triggers.
ipm.treatmentListIntro: Treatments include cultural, biological, chemical and mechanical measures against pests and diseases. Chemical treatments have safety intervals.
ipm.pestCreateIntro: Create a new pest entry. Enter the scientific and common name along with biological data.
ipm.diseaseCreateIntro: Create a new disease entry. Specify the pathogen type, incubation period and environmental triggers.
ipm.treatmentCreateIntro: Create a new treatment entry. Select the treatment type and specify dosage and safety interval.
ipm.scientificNameHelper: Binomial name of the pest (e.g. 'Tetranychus urticae')
ipm.lifecycleDaysHelper: Duration of the complete lifecycle in days
ipm.optimalTempHelper: Optimal temperature range for development and reproduction
ipm.incubationPeriodDaysHelper: Time from infection to symptom appearance
ipm.environmentalTriggersHelper: Environmental conditions that favor outbreak (press Enter to add)
ipm.affectedPlantPartsHelper: Affected parts: leaf, stem, root, flower (press Enter to add)
ipm.treatmentNameHelper: Name of the treatment or product
ipm.safetyIntervalDaysHelper: Waiting period between last application and harvest (required for chemical treatments)
ipm.dosagePerLiterHelper: Recommended dosage in ml per liter of water
ipm.protectiveEquipmentHelper: Required protective equipment (press Enter to add)
ipm.karenzSafe: No active safety intervals. Harvest is safe.
ipm.karenzActive: {{count}} active safety interval(s). Harvest is currently not safe.

harvest.title: Harvest Batches
harvest.listIntro: Harvest batches document the harvesting process from wet weight to quality assessment and yield analysis. Click a batch for details.
harvest.createIntro: Record a new harvest batch. Select the plant and enter the harvest data.
harvest.editIntro: Edit the data for this harvest batch. Weights and quality grade can be updated after creation.
harvest.createQualityIntro: Assess the quality of this harvest batch based on appearance, aroma, and color.
harvest.defectsHelper: Enter observed defects (press Enter to add)

phaseDescription.germination: Germination phase: Seeds absorb water, crack open and form the first root (radicle). Consistent moisture and warmth (20–25 °C) are critical. No fertilizer needed.
phaseDescription.seedling: Seedling phase: First cotyledons and true leaves develop. Very sensitive to overwatering and direct light. Low EC values (0.4–0.8 mS/cm).
phaseDescription.vegetative: Vegetative phase: Vigorous stem and leaf growth. High nitrogen demand (N), longer light periods promote growth. Pruning and training possible during this phase.
phaseDescription.flowering: Flowering phase: Formation of flowers/fruits. Switch to phosphorus/potassium-heavy feeding (P/K). In photoperiodic plants triggered by changing light cycles.
phaseDescription.ripening: Ripening phase: Fruits/flowers mature and reach harvest readiness. Reduce feeding. Harvest timing determined by fruit color, firmness and variety-specific ripeness indicators.
phaseDescription.harvest: Harvest phase: Plants are harvested. Observe safety intervals (IPM treatments). Perform harvest indicator checks and quality assessment.
phaseDescription.drying: Drying phase: Dry harvest material at 18–22 °C and 55–65% humidity. Slow drying improves quality and shelf life.
phaseDescription.curing: Curing phase: Store dried material in suitable containers, opening regularly for air exchange. Improves aroma and shelf life over several weeks.
phaseDescription.flushing: Flushing phase: Only plain water, no nutrients. Removes salt and nutrient residues from the substrate. Typically 1–2 weeks before harvest.
phaseDescription.juvenile: Juvenile phase: Young plant establishing itself, not yet able to flower. Building root system and basic structure. For perennials often 1–3 years.
phaseDescription.climbing: Climbing phase: Plant forms tendrils/shoots and needs support structure (trellis, net). Regular guiding of shoots and aerial roots promotes orderly growth.
phaseDescription.mature: Mature/productive phase: Plant is fully grown and producing full yield. Focus on maintenance, fertilization and harvest. Recurring annually for perennials.
phaseDescription.dormancy: Dormancy/winter rest: Plant reduces metabolism and growth. Greatly reduce watering, do not fertilize. Cool but frost-free location. Normal cycle for perennial plants.
phaseDescription.senescence: Senescence phase: Natural die-back of above-ground plant parts at season end. Nutrients withdraw into roots/tubers. Remove dead foliage, prepare plant for overwintering.
phaseDescription.establishment: Establishment phase: Plant acclimates to its new location and develops initial roots. Keep evenly moist, avoid direct sunlight.
phaseDescription.pup_establishment: Pup establishment: Separated pup roots slowly in its own pot. High humidity and warmth are essential.
phaseDescription.pre_bloom: Pre-bloom: Plant prepares for flower formation. Maintain nutrients and light levels.
phaseDescription.recovery: Recovery phase: Plant regenerates after harvest, building new leaf mass. Fertilize moderately and maintain care.
phaseDescription.sprouting: Sprouting: Tubers or bulbs sprout and form initial shoots. Water sparingly and keep warm.
phaseDescription.hardening_off: Hardening off: Gradually acclimate pre-grown plants to outdoor conditions. Only plant out permanently after last frost.
phaseDescription.budding: Budding: Plant forms flower buds. Phosphorus-rich feeding supports development.
phaseDescription.corm_ripening: Corm ripening: Plant transfers nutrients into the corm for next season. Reduce watering.
phaseDescription.tuber_formation: Tuber formation: Underground tuber swells. Potassium-rich feeding and consistent moisture are important.

chlorophytum_comosum.establishment: Acclimate freshly potted spider plant to its new location. Prefers indirect light, keep substrate evenly moist but avoid waterlogging.
chlorophytum_comosum.vegetative: Main growth phase with vigorous leaf development and increasing runner production. Fertilize regularly and repot when roots grow out of the pot.
chlorophytum_comosum.flowering: Spider plant produces long flower stalks with small white star-shaped blooms followed by plantlets. Don't cut runners — the plantlets can be used for propagation.
chlorophytum_comosum.mature: Fully grown, low-maintenance spider plant with high stress tolerance. Water moderately and fertilize occasionally — the plant easily tolerates short dry periods.

guzmania_lingulata.pup_establishment: Separated Guzmania pups root slowly in their own pot. High humidity and warmth are crucial — water into the leaf rosette rather than the substrate.
guzmania_lingulata.vegetative: Long growth phase of the Guzmania rosette — the plant accumulates energy over months for its single bloom. Fertilize very sparingly, refresh water in the rosette regularly.
guzmania_lingulata.pre_bloom: The Guzmania prepares for its one-time bloom. A ripe apple placed near the plant can trigger flowering through ethylene release.
guzmania_lingulata.flowering: The brilliantly colored bract bloom of the Guzmania lasts up to three months. Avoid direct sunlight as it bleaches the bract color.
guzmania_lingulata.senescence: The mother plant slowly dies back and produces 1–3 pups at its base. Only separate pups when they reach at least one-third the size of the mother plant.

monstera_deliciosa.juvenile: Young Monstera with unfenestrated, heart-shaped leaves. Bright location without direct sun promotes growth — install a support pole early.
monstera_deliciosa.vegetative: Monstera develops first fenestrated leaves and aerial roots. Mist regularly with lime-free water and guide aerial roots into moist moss on the support pole.
monstera_deliciosa.climbing: Monstera actively climbs, producing increasingly larger, more fenestrated leaves. Stable climbing support needed — keep moss pole or coir pole consistently moist.
monstera_deliciosa.mature: Fully grown Monstera with impressive leaves up to 90 cm diameter. Dust leaves regularly and prune as needed — cuttings are excellent for propagation.

spathiphyllum_wallisii.establishment: Acclimate freshly potted peace lily to a semi-shaded location. Sensitive to drafts and direct sun — drooping leaves indicate water needs immediately.
spathiphyllum_wallisii.vegetative: Peace lily forms strong dark green leaves and expands through division in the pot. Regular misting with soft water supports its tropical origins.
spathiphyllum_wallisii.flowering: The white spathe typically appears following cool night temperatures (15–17 °C) or drought stress. Cooler nights over several weeks can trigger bloom formation. Cut spent flower stalks close to the base.
spathiphyllum_wallisii.mature: Robust, mature peace lily with regular blooming. Propagate by dividing the root ball when needed — ideal during spring repotting.

fragaria_x_ananassa.germination: Strawberry seeds germinate slowly with consistent moisture and light. Press seeds onto surface only (light germinator) and cover with film until cotyledons appear.
fragaria_x_ananassa.vegetative: Vigorous leaf growth and runner production. Remove runners for stronger fruit harvest, or allow to root for propagation.
fragaria_x_ananassa.flowering: Strawberry blooms white and requires pollination for fruit set. Hand-pollinate with a brush for indoor growing — cooler night temperatures promote fruit set.
fragaria_x_ananassa.ripening: Fruits ripen and turn red — potassium-rich feeding promotes flavor and sweetness. Place straw or mulch underneath to prevent gray mold on resting fruits.
fragaria_x_ananassa.recovery: After harvest the strawberry plant regenerates and builds new leaf mass. Cut back old foliage and fertilize moderately to strengthen the plant for next season.
fragaria_x_ananassa.dormancy: Strawberry needs a cold period near 0–5 °C for flower induction next season. Water minimally and don't fertilize — provide frost protection for container plants.

helianthus_annuus.germination: Sunflower seeds germinate quickly with warmth and darkness. Plant seeds 2–3 cm deep and keep evenly moist — slugs are the biggest threat at this stage.
helianthus_annuus.seedling: Young sunflower develops first true leaf pairs and grows rapidly. Very light-hungry — insufficient light causes the stem to etiolate and break easily later.
helianthus_annuus.vegetative: Explosive height growth of the sunflower with high water and nutrient demand. Set support stake early for tall varieties, as the stem can reach up to 3 m.
helianthus_annuus.flowering: The flower head opens. Mature heads are stationary, facing east — heliotropism occurs only during the juvenile vegetative phase. High water demand — drought causes outer petals to wilt first and impairs seed formation.
helianthus_annuus.ripening: The flower head droops and seeds ripen. Apply bird protection netting if harvesting seeds — watering can now be significantly reduced.
helianthus_annuus.senescence: The annual sunflower dries out and can be composted. Hang flower heads with ripe seeds to dry — usable as seed stock or bird feed.

viola_x_wittrockiana.germination: Germinate pansy seeds at cool 14–16 °C in darkness. Cover the fine seeds only very thinly with soil and keep evenly moist.
viola_x_wittrockiana.seedling: Young pansies form compact leaf rosettes. Cool temperatures around 12–16 °C promote compact growth — warmth quickly causes etiolation.
viola_x_wittrockiana.vegetative: The pansy builds a strong leaf rosette and branches out. Regular pinching promotes bushy growth and more flower buds.
viola_x_wittrockiana.flowering: Long blooming period with the characteristic colorful flower faces. Regularly deadhead to maintain flower production for months.
viola_x_wittrockiana.senescence: In summer heat above 25 °C flowering diminishes and the plant declines. Cutting back hard and keeping cool can encourage fall reblooming in biennial varieties.

dahlia_pinnata.dormancy: Store dahlia tubers frost-free at 4–7 °C in dry sand or peat. Check regularly for rot and dehydration — lightly moisten shriveled tubers.
dahlia_pinnata.sprouting: Plant dahlia tubers in pots from April and provide warmth for sprouting. Water sparingly at first, increasing once the first green shoots appear.
dahlia_pinnata.hardening_off: Gradually acclimate pre-grown dahlias to outdoor conditions. Only plant permanently outdoors after the last frost (mid-May) — late frost is lethal for dahlias.
dahlia_pinnata.vegetative: Rapid shoot growth with high nutrient demand. Remove side shoots early on large-flowered varieties (disbudding) to achieve fewer but more magnificent blooms.
dahlia_pinnata.flowering: Magnificent dahlia blooms from July until first frost. Consistently deadhead and feed weekly with potassium-rich fertilizer for continuous flowering.
dahlia_pinnata.senescence: After the first frost, let the above-ground foliage die back and cut 10 cm above ground. Tubers mature underground — carefully dig up only after 1–2 weeks.

petunia_x_hybrida.germination: Germinate tiny petunia seeds at constant 22–24 °C with light. Don't cover with soil (light germinator) — plastic wrap covering maintains needed humidity.
petunia_x_hybrida.seedling: Very slow-growing petunia seedlings need lots of light and moderate temperatures. Transplant individually into pots after pricking out and protect from drafts.
petunia_x_hybrida.vegetative: Petunia branches out and builds leaf mass. Pinching shoot tips promotes bushy growth — nitrogen-rich feeding for vigorous leaf growth.
petunia_x_hybrida.hardening_off: Gradually acclimate petunias to outdoor temperatures. Place outside during day, bring in at night — only leave permanently outdoors after last frost.
petunia_x_hybrida.flowering: Abundant continuous blooming from May to October with regular feeding. Remove sticky spent blooms and shelter from rain — wet flowers easily stick together.
petunia_x_hybrida.senescence: The annual petunia dies with the first frosts. Collect seeds from capsules for next season — though for hybrid varieties, purchasing new seed is usually worthwhile.

tigridia_pavonia.sprouting: Plant tiger flower corms 5 cm deep from April and water moderately. The corms sprout slowly — waterlogging quickly leads to corm rot.
tigridia_pavonia.vegetative: Tiger flower develops sword-shaped, iris-like leaves and a sturdy stem. Sunny, wind-sheltered location is ideal — support with stake if needed.
tigridia_pavonia.hardening_off: Gradually acclimate pre-grown tiger flowers to outdoor conditions. Only plant out permanently after last frost — the corms cannot tolerate frost.
tigridia_pavonia.budding: Tiger flower forms flower buds on long stems. Phosphorus-rich feeding supports bud formation — don't relocate as the plant is now sensitive.
tigridia_pavonia.flowering: Spectacular spotted individual blooms open in the morning and wilt the same day. Each plant produces many flowers in succession over weeks — don't remove spent stems.
tigridia_pavonia.corm_ripening: Tiger flower transfers nutrients into the corm for next season. Reduce watering and let foliage die back naturally — don't cut foliage prematurely.
tigridia_pavonia.dormancy: Dig up corms after die-back, dry and store frost-free at 10–12 °C. Store warmer than dahlias — tiger flower corms cannot tolerate temperatures below 8 °C.

apium_graveolens_var_rapaceum.germination: Celery germinates very slowly at 20–22 °C and needs light to germinate. Only press seeds onto surface, don't cover — patience required as germination takes up to 3 weeks.
apium_graveolens_var_rapaceum.seedling: Celery seedlings grow extremely slowly initially and need constant moisture. Avoid temperatures below 10 °C at all costs — cold triggers premature bolting.
apium_graveolens_var_rapaceum.vegetative: Celery develops strong leaf stalks and dense foliage. High water demand and nitrogen-rich feeding — outer leaf stalks can be used as soup greens.
apium_graveolens_var_rapaceum.tuber_formation: The celeriac tuber swells underground and needs lots of potassium. Hill up soil around the tuber and keep evenly moist — drought leads to woody, cracked tubers.
apium_graveolens_var_rapaceum.harvest: Harvest celeriac before the first hard frost when the tuber is fist-sized. Light frost improves flavor — tubers can be stored for months at 0–4 °C with high humidity.
